codeReview.summary: Comprehensive implementation of async peer coagent channels. The channel.go implementation is clean and thread-safe with elegant use of condition variables via Go channels. All 5 new tools (spawn_agent, post_message, read_messages, wait_for_message, close_agent) are properly registered and implemented. Old tools (send_turn, steer_session, list_sessions) completely removed from code. The runAgent goroutine properly manages peer agent lifecycle with context cancellation and cleanup. Tests are comprehensive with excellent coverage of message passing, cursor semantics, timeout behavior, concurrent access, and close handling. Build, test, and vet all pass. No blocking issues found.

issues.description: In runAgent error handling paths, when posting error messages to the channel fails (lines 348, 353, 361), the error is silently ignored with `_, _ = ch.Post(...)`. While acceptable for error paths, this could theoretically mask issues if the channel itself is in a bad state. Consider logging these failures if logging infrastructure is available.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly deviated from TDD procedure because feature was partially implemented from prior attempts. Worker noted: 'This worker inherited partially implemented feature files from earlier attempts, so the first task was to verify and finish that work rather than starting from an untouched baseline.' The go-worker skill should explicitly address how to handle inherited in-progress work.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.deviations[0]: step 'Write Tests First (TDD Red Phase)' was skipped because implementation already existed. Worker added supplemental tests instead of writing tests first. This is the correct approach for the situation but the skill doesn't guide workers on when to deviate from TDD.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The new peer agent pattern introduces a significant behavioral model: spawn_agent starts a background goroutine (runAgent) that continuously waits for channel messages, processes each with a new LLM turn, and posts responses back. This automatic message loop pattern is not documented in .factory/library/architecture.md, though it's a key architectural component of the async coagent system.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: internal/adapters/native/tools_coagent.go:342-386 — runAgent goroutine implements a continuous Wait loop that automatically responds to all non-self messages. This lifecycle (spawn → auto-loop → close) is novel to this feature and differs from the old synchronous send_turn pattern. No existing architecture docs describe this pattern.

summary: Feature implementation is complete and correct. All validation contract points (VAL-CHAN-001 through VAL-CHAN-004) are satisfied. The channel-based async messaging system properly replaces the synchronous coagent API. Thread safety is handled correctly throughout. Tests are comprehensive and pass. Build and test suite passes. One minor non-blocking observation about error handling in error paths. Two shared state observations: (1) go-worker skill should address handling inherited in-progress code, and (2) the new peer agent auto-loop pattern could be documented in architecture.md for future reference.
